One thing that might be worth highlighting here is that this PR makes the semantics of the Aztec `Encoder`/`AztecWriter` slightly different from QR/PDF417/DataMatrix in terms of character set handling: specifying `charset == null` or no encoding hint means "do not add any ECI code; compliant decoders should treat as ISO_8859_1 because that's what the spec says."

The QR decoder in particular differs from this…

https://github.com/zxing/zxing/blob/0cf3b9be71680f50c90a71ca26ce0d33664b0dd6/core/src/main/java/com/google/zxing/qrcode/decoder/DecodedBitStreamParser.java#L235-L244

_Changing_ the behavior of the QR decoder would probably break a _ton_ of software out there which relies on ZXing's behavior of guessing the encoding, so I'm certainly not suggesting it :sweat_smile:.

If anything, it _might_ be a good idea to add the _same_ encoding-guessing behavior for PDF417/DataMatrix/Aztec, in case there are broken encoders out there, which encode UTF-8 without ECI.

I definitely see the benefits of the trailing commas, but I'll make two arguments against. Wall of text to follow, but I don't have a strong opinion either way.

First, most of the code does not have a trailing comma, so it is inconsistent in the codebase. There's only a few in this commit that I found, which shows that the majority of them don't have a trailing comma. Regardless of preferred style it would be nice if it was consistent.

Second, other languages often don't allow trailing commas. This may seem trivial, but I'm currently porting as much of ZXing as I can to JavaScript, and the two languages are close enough that sometimes it's just a matter of tweaking some syntax and keywords. ~Trailing commas in arrays aren't allowed in JavaScript, though, so I hit that a few times because it's easy to not see visually when porting hundreds of lines~. So I'd make the argument that it makes the code ever so slightly more accessible for porting to other languages without the trailing commas. Obviously language-specific features shouldn't be avoided for this reason, but in this case its something trivial.

Anyway, not a strong opinion, but there's my rationale. I can remove the comma relate edits if you'd still like to keep the trailing commas.
